# RenderDoc in-application API header (vendored)

`renderdoc_app.h` is the official RenderDoc in-application API header — a single,
self-contained, MIT-licensed C header (the license banner is at the top of the
file itself). It declares `RENDERDOC_GetAPI` and the versioned
`RENDERDOC_API_1_6_0` struct used to drive programmatic frame captures from
`include/Render2D/Present/RenderDocCapture.hpp` (Stage 22E).

- **Source:** https://github.com/baldurk/renderdoc — `renderdoc/api/app/renderdoc_app.h`
- **Branch:** `v1.x` (the API struct is append-only / ABI-stable across the 1.x line)
- **Retrieved:** 2026-06-17
- **License:** MIT (see the header's own copyright banner)

## Why this is a plain vendored file, not a submodule

Unlike `third_party/sdl|freetype|harfbuzz|sheenbidi` (which build code), this is a
single public API header with no build and no library — RenderDoc is never linked.
At runtime `RenderDocCapture` only *attaches* to an **already-injected**
`renderdoc.dll` / `librenderdoc.so` (i.e. when the program is launched under
RenderDoc); when RenderDoc is absent, capture is a clean no-op. Vendoring the one
header — rather than adding a submodule — is the integration method RenderDoc
documents for the in-application API.

Do not edit this file. Bump it by re-fetching from the URL above and re-running the
Debug + Perf gates.
